title: Oenothera pringlei
taxonomy: Oenothera pringlei belongs to the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. It is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. Within the genus Oenothera, Oenothera pringlei is classified as a part of the Oenothera section. The species Oenothera pringlei is further divided into several subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological variations. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and distribution patterns of the plant species.
uses: Oenothera pringlei has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. Its roots and leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also known to have potential applications in wound healing and as an antiseptic. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Oenothera pringlei in modern medicine. Further studies are required to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses.

constituents: Oenothera pringlei is a species of evening primrose, and its active constituents have not been extensively researched.
